sourceMovie.title: White House Down
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit White House Down for the same reason it broke through in 2013: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Roland Emmerich keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Vantage Point
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If White House Down clicked for you, Vantage Point (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after White House Down?
faq.answer: Try Vantage Point, In the Line of Fire, Minority Report—each shares DNA with White House Down while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is White House Down?
faq.answer: White House Down (2013) is best described as Action, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is White House Down worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
